2604.03635.title: A Generative Foundation Model for Multimodal Histopathology
2604.03635.paper_summary: Accurate diagnosis and treatment of complex diseases require integrating histological, molecular, and clinical data, yet in practice these modalities are often incomplete owing to tissue scarcity, assay cost, and workflow constraints. Existing computational approaches attempt to impute missing modalities from available data but rely on task-specific models trained on narrow, single source-target pairs, limiting their generalizability. Here we introduce MuPD (Multimodal Pathology Diffusion), a generative foundation model that embeds hematoxylin and eosin (H&E)-stained histology, molecular RNA profiles, and clinical text into a shared latent space through a diffusion transformer with decoupled cross-modal attention. Pretrained on 100 million histology image patches, 1.6 million text-histology pairs, and 10.8 million RNA-histology pairs spanning 34 human organs, MuPD supports diverse cross-modal synthesis tasks with minimal or no task-specific fine-tuning. For text-conditioned and image-to-image generation, MuPD synthesizes histologically faithful tissue architectures, reducing Fréchet inception distance (FID) scores by 50% relative to domain-specific models and improving few-shot classification accuracy by up to 47% through synthetic data augmentation. For RNA-conditioned histology generation, MuPD reduces FID by 23% compared with the next-best method while preserving cell-type distributions across five cancer types. As a virtual stainer, MuPD translates H&E images to immunohistochemistry and multiplex immunofluorescence, improving average marker correlation by 37% over existing approaches. These results demonstrate that a single, unified generative model pretrained across heterogeneous pathology modalities can substantially outperform specialized alternatives, providing a scalable computational framework for multimodal histopathology.
2604.03635.paper_summary_zh: 準確診斷和治療複雜疾病需要整合組織學、分子和臨床數據，然而在實際操作中，由於組織稀缺、檢測成本和工作流程限制，這些模式往往不完整。現有的計算方法試圖從可用數據中推測缺失的模式，但依賴於在狹窄的單一源-目標對上訓練的特定任務模型，限制了其通用性。在此，我們介紹MuPD（多模態病理擴散），這是一個生成基礎模型，通過具有解耦交叉模態注意力的擴散Transformer，將蘇木精-伊紅（H&E）染色的組織學、分子RNA檔案和臨床文本嵌入到共享潛在空間中。MuPD在1億個組織學圖像補丁、160萬個文本-組織學對和1080萬個RNA-組織學對上進行預訓練，涵蓋34個人類器官，支持多樣的交叉模態合成任務，幾乎不需要或不需要特定任務的微調。對於文本條件和圖像到圖像的生成，MuPD合成了組織學上真實的組織結構，將Fréchet起始距離（FID）分數相對於特定領域模型降低了50%，並通過合成數據增強將少量樣本分類準確率提高了最多47%。對於RNA條件的組織學生成，MuPD將FID相比於次佳方法降低了23%，同時保持了五種癌症類型的細胞類型分佈。作為一個虛擬染色劑，MuPD將H&E圖像轉換為免疫組織化學和多重免疫螢光，將平均標記相關性提高了37%，超過現有方法。這些結果表明，單一的統一生成模型在異質病理模態上進行預訓練，可以顯著超越專門的替代方案，提供一個可擴展的計算框架以進行多模態組織病理學。
2604.03635.author: Jinxi Xiang et.al.
2604.03635.authors: Jinxi Xiang, Mingjie Li, Siyu Hou, Yijiang Chen, Xiangde Luo, Yuanfeng Ji, Xiang Zhou, Ehsan Adeli, Akshay Chaudhari, Curtis P. Langlotz, Kilian M. Pohl, Ruijiang Li
